versions.note: Split by subject, so this is generalization to new people

inProgress: v2 — testing whether the signal is robust: band-limited inputs, per-channel ablation (which electrodes carry the signal?), and a larger held-out set via cross-validation to tighten the confidence interval.

changed: First end-to-end build: a 193k-parameter 1D CNN over all 19 EEG channels, classifying 2-second windows as ADHD vs control.
Split strictly by subject (train / validation / test share no people), and scored at the subject level by averaging each subject's window predictions.
Exported to self-contained ONNX (775 KB) for the on-device browser demo.
improved: Strong, honest baseline: 91.7% accuracy and 0.965 ROC-AUC on 24 completely unseen subjects — the model generalises to new people, not just new windows.
Unlike the ECG project, no big lab-to-reality gap appeared here: validation and test agree closely.
knownGaps: The test set is only 24 subjects, so 91.7% means 22 of 24 — a wide confidence interval. And this classifies research-cohort group membership, never a diagnosis.
